chief_complaint: A captive senile talapoin monkey (Miopithecus talapoin) developed sudden neurological signs.
history: The monkey was found to have sudden neurological signs and died within 24 hours. The necropsy revealed an extensive infarct in the right hemisphere, from occipital to frontal lobe.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings were not provided.
labs: Necropsy and microscopy showed a thrombus, neuronal edema, and necrosis, and arteriosclerosis.
